topics.title: Basic Sentence Structure
topics.explanation:
Bulgarian sentences follow Subject-Verb-Object (SVO) order, similar to English. However, word order is more flexible than in English because nouns are marked for case through context and articles.

The definite article in Bulgarian is attached to the END of the word (postpositive), not placed before it.

examples.english: The book is interesting. (definite article -та attached)

quiz.question: How is the definite article positioned in Bulgarian?
options: Attached to the END of the noun
It does not exist in Bulgarian
It is a separate word in the middle

quiz.question: What is the basic word order in Bulgarian?

topics.title: Noun Gender
topics.explanation:
Bulgarian nouns have three grammatical genders: masculine, feminine, and neuter.

• Masculine nouns typically end in a consonant: стол (chair), град (city)
• Feminine nouns typically end in -а or -я: жена (woman), земя (earth)
• Neuter nouns typically end in -о or -е: дете (child), слово (word)

Adjectives must agree in gender, number, and definiteness with the noun they modify.

examples.target: голям град (m) · голяма жена (f) · голямо дете (n)
examples.transliteration: golyam grad · golyama zhena · golyamo dete
examples.english: big city (m) · big woman (f) · big child (n)

examples.target: нов стол · нова книга · ново куче
examples.transliteration: nov stol · nova kniga · novo kuche
examples.english: new chair (m) · new book (f) · new dog (n)

quiz.question: Which ending is typical for neuter nouns in Bulgarian?

topics.title: Personal Pronouns
topics.explanation:
Bulgarian personal pronouns change form based on their function in the sentence.

Subject pronouns:
• аз – I
• ти – you (singular)
• той – he
• тя – she
• то – it
• ние – we
• вие – you (plural/formal)
• те – they

Note: Subject pronouns are often dropped in Bulgarian when the verb form makes the subject clear.

topics.title: The Verb "to be" (съм)
topics.explanation:
The verb "съм" (to be) is one of the most important verbs in Bulgarian.

Present tense conjugation:
• аз съм – I am
• ти си – you are
• той/тя/то е – he/she/it is
• ние сме – we are
• вие сте – you are (plural)
• те са – they are

Negative form: add "не" before the verb (не съм, не си, не е...)

quiz.question: How do you say "they are" in Bulgarian?

topics.title: Present Tense Verb Conjugation
topics.explanation:
Bulgarian verbs are conjugated based on person and number. There are three conjugation groups.

Example – говоря (to speak):
• аз говоря – I speak
• ти говориш – you speak
• той говори – he speaks
• ние говорим – we speak
• вие говорите – you (pl) speak
• те говорят – they speak

quiz.question: How do you say "we speak" using говоря?

topics.title: Past Tense (Минало свършено)
topics.explanation:
The simple past tense in Bulgarian is used to describe completed actions in the past.

It is formed from the past stem + personal endings:
• аз говорих – I spoke
• ти говори – you spoke
• той говори – he spoke
• ние говорихме – we spoke
• вие говорихте – you (pl) spoke
• те говориха – they spoke

quiz.question: Which sentence is in the past tense?

topics.title: Future Tense
topics.explanation:
The future tense in Bulgarian is formed using the particle "ще" + the present tense form of the verb.

Formation: ще + present tense verb
• Аз ще говоря – I will speak
• Ти ще говориш – You will speak

Negative future: "няма да" + present tense
• Аз няма да говоря – I will not speak

examples.english: Tomorrow I will go to work.

quiz.question: How is the future tense formed in Bulgarian?
How do you say "I will not go" in Bulgarian?
